variantrule.name: Weapon Prowess
entries: The New Feature that provides more options and dynamic range of tactical and powerful abilities to martial classes - {@class Barbarian|PHB}, {@class Fighter|PHB}, {@class Monk|PHB}, {@class Paladin|PHB}, {@class Ranger|PHB}, and {@class Rogue|PHB}

The more you use a weapon, the more techniques you learn. When you reach level 5 in one of the above classes, choose one weapon (or two if you reach level 5 as a fighter, paladin, or a ranger), you unlock the Prowess properties of that weapon. On hit, once per turn, you may choose to activate Prowess if the conditions meet. When you reach a level, you may change one weapon for Prowess.

entries.name: {@bold Weapon Prowess Requirements}
entries: You must be Proficient with the weapon used to make the attack roll.
You can only use Prowess Ability once per turn.
The Prowess ability requires the concerned attack roll to cause weapon’s primary damage. If the target is immune to the primary damage of the weapon, Prowess ability does not activate.
Critical Hit doesn’t guarantee Prowess Ability activation if the Attack Roll is not higher than Prowess threshold against Target's Armor Class.

entries.name: Breach
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Spear|PHB}, {@item Javelin|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s speed is halved until the end of their next turn.

Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target’s speed is halved until the end of their next turn and they take further 1d6 piercing damage on willingly moving 5 feet until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Bash
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Maul|PHB}, {@item Lance|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker hits by rolling five or higher than Target’s AC, the Target is pushed 5 feet away from the attacker in any direction of their choice.

Starting at level 11, if the attacker hits by rolling ten or higher than Target’s AC, the Target is instead either pushed 10 feet away from the attacker in any direction of their choice or falls prone if the attacker choose.

Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Bash prowess, the target must also make a Constitution Saving Throw ( DC 8 + Ability Modifier Used to Attack + Proficiency Bonus), on fail the target is Dazed until the end of their next turn. On a success their movement is halved until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Feint
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Battleaxe|PHB}, {@item Greataxe|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the target has disadvantage on their next attack roll until the end of their next turn.

Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the target has disadvantage on their next attack roll until the end of their next turn. The target must also make a strength saving throw against Attacker’s prowess DC or is disarmed of any weapon, shield, or focus held by one hand only. The concerned object is yanked 5 feet behind the attacker.
Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Feint prowess, the next attack roll before the end of attacker’s next turn is considered critical on a natural 15 or higher.

entries.name: Flourish
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Greatsword|PHB}, {@item Longsword|PHB}, {@item Double-Bladed Scimitar|ERLW}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the attacker gains +2 AC until the start of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the attacker gains +5 AC until the start of their next turn.

Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Flourish prowess, any magical attack roll that misses against the attacker is deflected back at the caster and hits if the original attack roll meets or exceeds the caster’s armor class.

entries.name: Lacerate
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Dagger|PHB}, {@item Sickle|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more for a melee attack, the Target has bleeding condition and loses 1d4 hp at the start of their turn, until healed.

Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more for a melee attack, the Target has bleeding condition and loses 1d8 hp at the start of their turn instead, until healed.

entries.name: Pummel
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Greatclub|PHB}, {@item Flail|PHB}, {@item Morningstar|PHB}, {@item Mace|PHB}, {@item Warhammer|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target’s speed is reduced by 20 feet until the end of their next turn.

Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Pummel prowess, the target must also make a Constitution Saving Throw ( DC 8 + Ability Modifier Used to Attack + Proficiency Bonus), on fail the target is Incapacitated until the end of their next turn. On a success, the next attack against the target can be rolled with advantage.

entries.name: Puncture
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Heavy Crossbow|PHB}, {@item War Pick|PHB}, {@item Pike|PHB}, {@item Halberd|PHB}, {@item Trident|PHB}, {@item Glaive|PHB}

Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s AC is reduced by 1. The damage can only be restored by mending in case of armor or shield and healing in case of natural armor or unarmored defence.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target’s AC is reduced by 2 instead.

Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Puncture prowess, the target must also make a Strength Saving Throw (DC 8 + Ability Modifier Used to Attack + Proficiency Bonus), on fail the target is bleeding and suffers 2 points of piercing damage at the start of their turn until healed.

entries.name: Stagger
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Club|PHB}, {@item Light Hammer|PHB}

Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s passive perception is reduced by 5 and they have disadvantage on perception checks until the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target has Blinded condition until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Tangle
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Club|PHB}, {@item Light Hammer|PHB}

Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s passive perception is reduced by 5 and they have disadvantage on perception checks until the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target has Blinded condition until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Torpor
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Handaxe|PHB}, {@item Hand Crossbow|PHB}, {@item Light Crossbow|PHB}, {@item Shortbow|PHB}, {@item Longbow|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target must subtract a d4 from their next attack roll before the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target must subtract a d8 instead from their next attack roll before the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Torpor prowess, all attack rolls against the Target have advantage until the end of their next turn.

entries.name: Twirl
entries: {@bold Weapons:} {@item Rapier|PHB}, {@item Shortsword|PHB}, {@item Scimitar|PHB}
Starting at level 5, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 5 or more, the Target’s next attack roll doesn’t include their attack’s ability modifier until the end of their next turn.
Starting at level 11, if the attacker’s hit roll exceeds Target’s AC by 10 or more, the Target is also disarmed of their weapon or spell focus.

Starting at level 17, if a hit activates the Twirl prowess, the target must also make an Athletics or Acrobatics Check (DC 8 + Ability Modifier Used to Attack + Proficiency Bonus) as a reaction, on fail the target is grappled and restrained by the attacker.

subclass.name: Path of the World Tree
subclassFeatures: Path of the World Tree|Barbarian|PHB|World Tree|JaysGuideToMaa|3

subclassFeature.name: Cartographer
entries: {@i Chart Advantageous Courses through Turmoil}
Cartographers are the premier navigators and reconnaissance agents. Using their creations, Cartographers can highlight threats, safeguard allies, and carve portals to distant locations.

subclassFeature.name: Tools of the Trade
entries: {@i 3rd-level Cartographer feature}
You gain the following benefits.

{@b Tool Proficiency.} You gain proficiency with {@item calligrapher's supplies|PHB} and {@item cartographer's tools|PHB}. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item artisan's tools|PHB} of your choice (or with two other types if you have both).
{@b Scroll Crafting.} When you scribe a {@item Spell Scroll|DMG} using the crafting rules in the {@book Player’s Handbook|PHB}, the amount of time required to craft it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Cartographer Spells
entries: {@i 3rd-level Cartographer feature}
When you reach an Artificer level specified in the Cartographer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Cartographer Spells
rows: {@spell faerie fire}, {@spell guiding bolt}, {@spell healing word}

subclassFeature.name: Adventure's Atlas
entries: {@i 3rd-level Cartographer feature}

Whenever you finish a Long Rest while holding {@item cartographer's tools|PHB}, you can use that tool to create a set of magical maps by touching at least two creatures (one of whom can be yourself), up to a maximum number of creatures equal to 1 plus your Intelligence modifier (minimum of two creatures). Each target receives a magical map, which constantly updates to show the relative position of all the map holders but is illegible to all others. The maps last until you die or until you use this feature again, at which point any existing maps created by this feature immediately vanish.
While carrying the map, a target gains the following benefits.
{@b Awareness.} The target adds {@dice 1d4} to its Initiative rolls.

{@b Positioning.} The target knows the location of all other map holders that are on the same plane of existence as itself. When casting a spell or creating another effect that requires being able to see the effect’s target, a map holder can target another map holder regardless of sight or cover, so long as the other map holder is still within the effect’s range.

subclassFeature.name: Mapping Magic
entries: {@i 3rd-level Cartographer feature}
You gain the following benefits.

{@b Illuminated Cartography.}  You can cast {@spell Faerie Fire|PHB} without expending a spell slot, outlining the affected creatures as if in ink. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

{@b Portal Jump.} On your turn, you can spend an amount of movement equal to half your Speed (round down) to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of yourself or within 5 feet of a creature that is within 30 feet of you and holding one of your Adventurer’s Atlas maps. You can’t use this benefit if your Speed is 0.

subclassFeature.name: Guided Precision
entries: {@i 5th-level Cartographer feature}

Once per turn, whenever you cast a spell from your Cartographer Spells list or hit a creature affected by your {@spell Faerie Fire|PHB} with an attack roll, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of the spell or attack.
In addition, taking damage can’t cause you to lose {@status concentration|PHB} on {@spell Faerie Fire|PHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Ingenious Movement
entries: {@i 9th-level Cartographer feature}

When you use your {Flash of Genius|Artificer|TCE|7}, you or a willing creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of yourself can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that same Reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Atlas
entries: {@i 15th-level Cartographer feature}
Your Adventurer’s Atlas improves, gaining the following benefits.

{@b Safe Haven.} When a map holder would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, that creature can destroy its map. The creature’s Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Artificer level, and the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you or another map holder of its choice.

{@b Unerring Path.} If you are one of the map holders for your Adventurer’s Atlas, you can cast {@spell Find the Path|PHB} without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without needing spell components. Once you use this benefit, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the World Tree
entries: {@i Trace the Roots and Branches of the Multiverse}

Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree believe that their {Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} links them to the cosmic ash tree Yggdrasil. This 'world tree' grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and to the many worlds of the Material Plane, and the greatest plants on every world are said to be distant descendants of mighty Yggdrasil. These Barbarians draw on their connection to the world tree as a source of vitality and as a means of travel across the multiverse.

subclassFeature.name: Vitality of the Tree
entries: {@i 3rd-level Path of the World Tree feature}
Your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} taps into the lifeforce of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits:
{@b Vitality Surge.} When you activate your {Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Barbarian level.

{@b Life-Giving Force.} At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain {@quickref Temporary Hit Points}. To determine the number of Temporary Hit Points, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

subclassFeature.name: Branches of the Tree
entries: {@i 6th-level Path of the World Tree feature}

While your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} is active, whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equal to 8 plus your Proficiency Bonus plus your Strength modifier) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. The space the target teleports to must be on a surface or liquid that can support it; otherwise, the target doesn't teleport.
After the target teleports, you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

subclassFeature.name: Battering Roots
entries: {@i 10th-level Path of the World Tree feature}
Tendrils of the World Tree extend from your Melee weapons. During your turn, while you wield a Melee weapon with the Heavy or Versatile property, your reach with that weapon increases by 10 feet.

Additionally once per turn, when you hit with this weapon, you may force the target to make a constitution save (DC 8+Your Strength Modifier+Your Proficiency Bonus) on fail, you may choose to push the target 10 feet or fall {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Travel Along the Tree
entries: {@i 14th-level Path of the World Tree feature}

You can briefly travel along the World Tree. When you activate your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1} and as a Bonus Action while it's active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

In addition, when you teleport using this feature, you can bring up to six willing creatures who are within 10 feet of yourself, and the range you can teleport increases to 500 feet. Each creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of your destination space. You can teleport other creatures with you only once per {Rage|Barbarian|PHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: College of Dance
entries: {@i Move in Harmony with the Cosmos}

{@class Bard|PHB|Bards} of the College of Dance know that the Words of Creation can't be contained within speech or even song; they are uttered by the movements of celestial bodies and flow through the motions of even the smallest creatures. To these Bards, dance is art freed from the constraints of a single point in space and time; it is meaning unconfined by narrow definitions of words and structures of grammar. The College of Dance practices a way of being in harmony with the ever-whirling cosmos, emphasizing agility, speed, and grace.

subclassFeature.name: Dazzling Footwork
entries: {@i 3rd-level College of Dance feature}
Your practice of dance gives you the ability to gracefully dodge and make agile strikes. While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you gain the following benefits:

{@b Dance Virtuoso.} You have Advantage on any Charisma ({@skill Performance}) check you make that involves you dancing. On their discretion, the DM may also grant advantage on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) and Charisma ({@skill Deception}) Checks made with no or few words but heavily relying on expressions and gestures.
{@b Unarmored Defense.} Your base Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

{@b Agile Strikes.} When you expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} as part of an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction, you can make one Unarmed Strike as part of that action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.

{@b Bardic Damage.} You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack rolls of your Unarmed Strikes, and when you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to a roll of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB} die plus your Dexterity modifier instead of the strike's normal damage. This roll doesn't expend the die.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Movement
entries: {@i 6th-level College of Dance feature}

When an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 feet of an ally of yours who is within 60 feet of you, you can use your Reaction and expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} to move up to half your Speed. Then roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and the ally can move up to a number of feet equal to 5 times the number rolled. None of this feature's movement provokes {@action Opportunity Attack}.

subclassFeature.name: Tandem Footwork
entries: {@i 6th-level College of Dance feature}

When you roll Initiative, you can spend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} if you don't have the Incapacitated condition. When you do so, roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of yourself (you can choose yourself), up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of them gains a bonus to their Initiative equal to the number rolled.

subclassFeature.name: Leading Evasion
entries: {@i 14th-level College of Dance feature}

When you roll Initiative, you can spend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} if you don't have the {@condition incapacitated} condition. When you do so, roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of yourself (you can choose yourself), up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). Each of them gains a bonus to their Initiative equal to the number rolled.

subclassFeature.name: College of Moon
entries: {@i Inspire Allies with Primal Tales}

The College of the Moon traces its origins to the ancient druidic circles of night, who entrusted the first Bards of this tradition with chronicling the stories of moon worshippers and their connection to feywild. Bards of this college draw from the fey magic and the primal power of the moonwells to bolster their allies, protect the natural world, and inspire their bardic works.

subclassFeature.name: Moon's Inspiration
entries: {@i 3rd-level College of Moon feature}
The primal and ever-changing power of the moon flows through you, granting you the following benefits.

{@b Inspired Eclipse.}  When you take a Bonus Action to give a creature a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} die, you can have the {@condition invisible} condition and teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that Bonus Action. This invisibility lasts until the start of your next turn and ends early immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.

{@b Lunar Vitality.} Once per turn when you restore Hit Points to a creature with a spell, you can expend a Bardic Inspiration die and increase the amount of Hit Points restored by a number equal to a roll of the Bardic Inspiration die. The creature's Speed also increases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Lore
entries: {@i 3rd-level College of Moon feature}

You learn {@language Druidic|PHB} and one cantrip from the Druid spell list. It counts as a Bard spell for you but doesn't count against the number of cantrips you know. Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace this cantrip with another cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Druid spell list|spells|level=0|class=druid}.

Additionally, choose one of the following skills: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Medicine}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Perception}, or {@skill Survival}. You have proficiency in that skill.

subclassFeature.name: Blessing of Moonlight
entries: {@i 6th-level College of Moon feature}
You always have the {@spell Moonbeam} spell prepared.

When you cast {@spell Moonbeam}, you can modify the spell so that you glow faintly while the spell is active. While glowing, you shed dim light out to 5 feet, and whenever a creature fails its saving throw against the effects of this Moonbeam, another creature of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of yourself regains 2d4 Hit Points.
Once you use this feature to modify a casting of Moonbeam, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Eventide's Splendor
entries: {@i 14th-level College of Moon feature}
You become suffused with the might of the moon, improving your {@subclassFeature Moon's Inspiration|Bard|PHB|Moon|JaysGuideToMaa|3} in the following ways.

{@b Shadow of the New Moon.} When you use {@subclassFeature Moon's Inspiration|Bard|PHB|Moon|JaysGuideToMaa|3|Inspired Eclipse}, the creature who received the Bardic Inspiration die can also have the Invisible condition and immediately take a Reaction to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The creature remains Invisible until the start of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Improved College of Whispers
entries: {@i Divide and conquer your enemies with whispers of chaos}

Most folk are happy to welcome a bard into their midst. Bards of the College of Whispers use this to their advantage. They appear to be like other bards, sharing news, singing songs, and telling tales to the audiences they gather. In truth, the College of Whispers teaches its students that they are wolves among sheep. These bards use their knowledge and magic to uncover secrets and turn them against others through extortion and threats.

Many other bards hate the College of Whispers, viewing it as a parasite that uses a bard’s reputation to acquire wealth and power. For this reason, members of this college rarely reveal their true nature. They typically claim to follow some other college, or they keep their actual calling secret in order to infiltrate and exploit royal courts and other settings of power.

subclassFeature.name: Psychic Blades
entries: {@i 3rd-level Jay's Improved College of Whispers feature}
When you join the College of Whispers at 3rd level, you gain the ability to make your weapon attacks magically toxic to a creature’s mind.

When you hit a creature with a weapon {@action attack|PHB}, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} to deal an extra {@dice 2d6} psychic damage to that target. You can do so only once per round on your turn. While using Psychic Blades, You can also give yourself advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet.
In addition, on reaching level 14, any hit you score against a creature that is {@condition surprised} is a critical hit.
The psychic damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class, increasing to {@dice 3d6} at 5th level, {@dice 5d6} at 10th level, {@dice 8d6} at 15th level, and {@dice 10d6} at 19th level.

subclassFeature.name: Words of Terror
entries: {@i 3rd-level Jay's Improved College of Whispers feature}
At 3rd level, you learn to infuse innocent-seeming words with an insidious magic that can inspire terror. You gain Proficiency in {@skill Intimidation}, if you don't have it already.

If you speak to a creature alone for at least 1 minute, that can understand you, you can attempt to seed paranoia in its mind. At the end of the conversation, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be frightened of you or another creature of your choice. The target is frightened in this way for 1 hour, but can make the wisdom save again if it is attacked or damaged or until it witnesses its allies being attacked or damaged.
If the target succeeds on its saving throw, the target has no hint that you tried to frighten it.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Mantle of Whispers
entries: {@i 6th-level Jay's Improved College of Whispers feature}

At 6th level, you gain the ability to adopt a humanoid’s persona. When a humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can magically capture its shadow using your reaction. You retain this shadow until you use it or target's death is discovered by anyone except your allies.

You can use the shadow as an action to gain access to all information that the humanoid would freely share with a casual acquaintance. Such information includes general details on its background and personal life, but doesn’t include secrets. The information is enough that you can pass yourself off as the person by drawing on its memories.
Another creature can see through this disguise by succeeding on a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by your Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check. You gain a +5 bonus to your check.
{@b Identity Theft.} You also learn Disguise Self spell, you can cast this spell once without using a spell slot before finishing a short or long rest.
Once you capture a shadow with this feature, you can’t capture another one until you use the shadow and have finished a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Psychic Grasp
entries: {@i 6th-level Jay's Improved College of Whispers feature}

Starting at 6th level, your soft words and enchanting gaze can magically enthrall another creature. As an action, expend one {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|PHB|1} die to choose one creature that you can see within 5 feet of you. If the target can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your bard spell save DC or be frightened and grappled by you until the end of your next turn. The frightened creature is also incapacitated and visibly dazed.

On subsequent turns, you can use your action to maintain this effect, extending its duration until the end of your next turn. However, the effect ends if you move more than 5 feet away from the creature, if the creature can neither see nor hear you, or if the creature takes damage.

On reaching 14th level, you can leverage the safety of your hostage against their allies. As a bonus action, you command a course of activity (limited to a sentence or two) and magically influence up to six creatures of your choice that you can see within range and that can hear and understand you. Creatures that can't be {@condition charmed} are immune to this effect. The command must be worded in such a manner as to make the course of action sound reasonable. Asking the creature to perform obviously harmful act to themselves or anybody else automatically negates the effect.
Once the effect ends, or if the creature succeeds on its initial saving throw against this effect, you can't use this feature on that creature again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Wormtongue
entries: {@i 14th-level Jay's Improved College of Whispers feature}
At 14th level, you gain the ability to exploit the trust others bestow upon you.

As an action, choose a creature within 30 feet of you that isn't hostile towards you. You magically whisper a phrase forcing the target to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. It automatically succeeds if it can not hear you or understand you. On a successful saving throw, your whisper sounds like unintelligible mumbling and has no effect.

On a failed saving throw, the target is {@condition charmed} by you for the next 8 hours or until you or your allies attack it, damage it, or force it to make a saving throw. Paranoia clouds the targets thoughts and they begin to distrust everyone around them without reason except you. They grant you favors and gifts they would offer to a close friend. They will also value your opinions, suggestions, and advise in highest regards and may share its darkest, closely held secrets with you.

The target won't risk their life or status for you or fight for you, unless they were already inclined to do so and can choose to ignore your suggestions if they find them unreasonable despite the paranoia.

You can choose to extend the effect for another 8 hours duration at the end of the effect, forcing the target to roll the saving throw at disadvantage. Unless the target is attacked, taken damage or forced to make a saving throw by you or your allies, or they are aided by another creature to end the effect, the target doesn't remember that they were charmed.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest and you can only charm one person this way at a time.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Genie's Splendor
entries: {@i Brandish the Elemental Splendor of Genies}

Swore an oath of fealty to a noble djinn, the aristocrats of the Elemental Planes. Through taking this oath, Paladins draw power from one of the four different types of genies — Dao, masters of earth; Djinni, masters of air; Efreeti, masters of fire; and Marid, masters of water. On Maa, many Paladins who swear this oath hail from either Hasea or Mithras, two regions to have most recognized presence of genies.
Paladins who swear this oath often undertake quests that take them all over the Realms and across the multiverse—including the Elemental Planes. These paladins share the following tenets:

items: Sow the seeds of creation amid the ashes of destruction.
Lead with splendor and grace.
Respect the elements, and fear their wrath.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Guard
entries: {@i 3rd-level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}
When you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers. You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.
You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Noble Fealty
entries: {@i 3rd-level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}
Paladins who take The Oath of Genies' Splendor swear fealty to a Noble Genie. Choose a noble — {@creature Dao}, {@creature Djinni}, {@creature Efreeti}, or {@creature Marid}.

items: {@creature Dao} are known for their material vanity and hoarding tendencies, Paladins that swore an allegiance to them are treasure hunters. Gemstones and precious metals determine their morality.
{@creature Djinni} are known for their beauty and kindness, Paladins that swore an allegiance to them believe in servitude for greater good. Generosity and helping each other guides their morality.

{@creature Efreeti} are known for their extremely short temper and drive for vengeance, Paladins that swore an allegiance to them believe in righteousness at any cost. Desire for revenge and wrath guides their morality.

{@creature Marid} are known for their love for creativity and deceptive web of lies, Paladins that swore an allegiance to them believe in living larger than lives. The ambition to become a legend and to have stories to tell guides their morality.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Smite
entries: {@i 3rd-level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}

Immediately after you cast {@classFeature Divine Smite|Paladin|PHB|2}, based on your Noble Fealty choice, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Paladin|PHB|3} and invoke one of the following effects.

items: {@b Dao's Crush.} Earth rises up around the target of your {@classFeature Divine Smite|Paladin|PHB|2}. The target has the {@condition Grappled} condition (escape DC equal to your spell save DC). While Grappled, the target has the {@condition Restrained} condition.

{@b Djinni's Escape.} You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of yourself and take on a semi-incorporeal form, which lasts until the end of your next turn. While in this form, you have resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you have Immunity to the {@condition Grappled}, {@condition Prone}, and {@condition Restrained} conditions.

{@b Efreeti's Fury.} The target of your {@classFeature Divine Smite|Paladin|PHB|2} takes an extra {@dice 2d4} Fire damage, and fire jumps from the target to another creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The second creature also takes {@dice 2d4} Fire damage.

{@b Marid's Surge.} A strong wave eminates from you forcing the target of your divine strike and each creature of your choice within 10-foot radius to make a Strength saving throw against your paladin spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature is pushed 15 feet straight away from you and has the {@condition Prone} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Genie Spells
entries: {@i 3rd-Level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}
Based on your noble fealty choice, when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Genie Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Splendor Spells
rows: {@spell Elementalism|JaysGuideToMaa}, {@spell Frost Fingers|IDRotF}, {@spell Create or Destroy Water}
{@spell Meld into Stone}, {@spell Wall of Sand|XGE}

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Endurance
entries: {@i 7th-Level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}
As a bonus action, choose one of the following damage types based on your Noble Fealty:

items: {@b Dao.} Poison, Thunder.
{@b Djinni.} Lightning, Thunder.
{@b Efreeti.} Fire, Cold.
{@b Marid.} Acid, Cold, Lightning.

entries: You and your allies, while they are with in your aura of protection, have resistance to your chosen damage type until you use a bonus action to change it.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Rebuke
entries: {@i 15th-Level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}

When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack's damage against yourself (round down) and force the attacker to make a Dexterity saving throw against your Paladin spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes damage equal to {@dice 2d10} plus your Charisma modifier of one of the following types (based on your {@subclassFeature Noble Fealty|PHB|Genie's Splendor|JaysGuideToMaa|3}): Force(Dao), Lightning(Djinni), Fire(Efreeti), Cold(Marid). On a successful save, the attacker takes half as much damage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Noble Scion
entries: {@i 20th-Level Oath of Genie's Splendor Feature}

As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits, based on your Noble Fealty, for 10 minutes or until you end them as a free action. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest or by expending a level 5 spell slot.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

items: {@b Dao.}
{@b Flight.} You have a Fly Speed of 30 feet and can hover.
{@b Burrow.} You have a Burrow Speed of 20 feet through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone.

{@b Minor Wish} When you or an ally in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|PHB|6} fails an ability skill, attack, or saving throw, you can take a Reaction to make that roll a success instead.
{@b Djinni.}
{@b Flight.} You have a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

{@b Minor Wish} When you or an ally in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|PHB|6} fails an ability skill, attack, or saving throw, you can take a Reaction to make that roll a success instead.
{@b Efreeti.}
{@b Flight.} You have a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

{@b Minor Wish} When you or an ally in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|PHB|6} fails an ability skill, attack, or saving throw, you can take a Reaction to make that roll a success instead.
{@b Marid.}
{@b Flight.} You have a Fly Speed of 30 feet and can hover.
{@b Swim.} You have a Swim Speed of 30 feet and can walk on water.

{@b Minor Wish} When you or an ally in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|PHB|6} fails an ability skill, attack, or saving throw, you can take a Reaction to make that roll a success instead.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Hunter

entries: If it bleeds, I can kill it!

Emulating the Hunter archetype means accepting your place as a bulwark between civilization and the terrors of the wilderness. As you walk the Hunter’s path, you learn specialized techniques for fighting the threats you face, from rampaging ogres and hordes of orcs to towering giants and terrifying dragons.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Prey
entries: {@i 3rd-Level Improved Hunter feature.}
At 3rd level, you gain two of the following features of your choice. You may only use one Hunter's Prey feature each round.

{@b Colossus Slayer.} Your tenacity can wear down the most potent foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature takes an extra {@dice 1d8} damage if it’s below its hit point maximum. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

{@b Giant Killer.} When a Large or larger creature within 5 feet of you attacks you, you can use your reaction to attack that creature immediately after its attack, provided that you can see the creature.

{@b Horde Breaker.} Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon action attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within range of your weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Lore
entries: {@i 3rd-Level Improved Hunter Feature.}

You have trained extensively for months and years to track, chase, and hunt the wild beasts that roam in the forests, soar in the sky, dwell into deep waters, and burrow within the rock and dirt. The years of experience has not only heightened your senses but also turned you into a font of knowledge regarding the bhaviour and nature of wild beasts. Any Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) Checks or Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) checks made to track a beast are to be rolled with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Defensive Tactics
entries: {@i 7th-Level Improved Hunter feature.}
At 7th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
{@b Escape the Horde.} When you are in two or more enemies' melee range, opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.
{@b Multiattack Defense.} When a creature hits you with an attack, you gain a +4 bonus to AC against all subsequent attacks made by that creature for the rest of the turn.
{@b Steel Will.} You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Hunter's Lore
entries: {@i 7th-Level Improved Hunter feature.}

You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your Hunter's Mark, you know whether that creature has any damage or condition immunities, damage resistances, or damage vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.

subclassFeature.name: Multiattack
entries: {@i 11th-Level Improved Hunter feature.}
At 11th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
{@b Volley.} At 11th level, you gain one of the following features of your choice.
{@b Whirlwind Attack.} You can use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Hunter's Mark
entries: {@i 11th-Level Improved Hunter feature.}
Once per turn, when you have Hunter's Mark on a creature and you deal damage to them, you can also deal the spell's extra damage to a different creature within 30 feet of the first creature.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
entries: {@i 15th-Level Improved Hunter feature.}
At 15th level, you gain two of the following features of your choice.

{@b Evasion.} You can nimbly dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a red dragon’s fiery breath or a lightning bolt spell. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

{@b Stand Against the Tide.} When a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to force that creature to repeat the same attack against another creature (other than itself), within its melee range, of your choice.
{@b Uncanny Dodge.} When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you.

subclassFeature.name: Sleuth

entries: I don't 'guess', I observe. And once I observe, I deduce.

Like any forest, Concrete Jungles need their watchers and hunters too. Unlike most rangers, you were born in the busy districts of a town or city and made their rooftops and dark alley your hunting grounds. You have a keen sense of urban life and the dangers it comes with, the crime bubbling right underneath the surface of an otherwise civilized society.

You can read a crime scene like a hunter tracks its prey, you have a knack for finding clues and evidence, skulk around to spy on people, and you learn going around settlements much quicker than your companions.

subclassFeature.name: Sleuth Magic
entries: {@i 3rd-level Sleuth feature}

Starting at 3rd level, you learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Sleuth Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

{@b {@i Ritual Practice.}} The inquisitive mind you possess has an innate curiosity about how magic works, even if its just in theory. You observed and learn to perform rituals to cast arcane spells that you have written down in your detective’s notebooks and you must have the notebook in casting while performing the ritual to cast a spell. At level 3, the notebook already has two additional {@filter 1st level Spells with ritual tag|spells|level=1|components & miscellaneous=ritual}. A spell written in the notebook counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of {@filter ranger spells|spells|class=ranger} you know. Your spellcasting ability for these spells is Wisdom.

If you come across a spell in written form, such as a {@item spell scroll|DMG|magical spell scroll} or a wizard’s spell book, you might be able to add it to your notebook. The spell’s level can be no higher than third your level (rounded up), and it must have the ritual tag. The process of copying the spell into your notebook takes 2 hours per level of the spell, and costs 50 gp per level. The cost represents material components you expend as you experiment with the spell to master it, as well as the fine inks you need to record it.
Education never ends, Dear Friend. It is a series of lessons, with the greatest for the last.

subclassFeature.name: Dissectologist
entries: {@i 3rd-level Sleuth feature}

You detest sitting idle, your mind is always looking for a problem to resolve, a puzzle to decipher, or elucidate a mystery. Your instincts, observation and experience makes up for for what you lack in theoretical knowledge.
Whenever you make an Intelligence check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).
In addition, you gain proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}.

subclassFeature.name: Battle Assessment
entries: {@i 3rd-level Sleuth feature}

Starting at 3rd level, you learn how to spot openings in your enemy’s moves and use it against them. When an enemy that you can see makes an {@action attack}, hit or miss, against you or your allies, you can use your reaction to target that enemy with a weapon attack, given that enemy is within range of the weapon attack.

subclassFeature.name: Beautiful Mind
entries: {@i 7th-level Sleuth feature}
Starting at 7th level, you have honed your mind to recognize patterns, recall details, or react to ever changing environment around you. You gain one of the following features:

entries.name: Astute Eye
entries: You quickly assess the environment around you. You gain a +5 bonus to your Initiative, passive Wisdom ({@skill Perception}), and passive Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) scores.

items: You can track time, direction, and detail with uncanny precision.

As an action, you can accurately recall anything you have seen or heard within the past year including a creature’s damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities if the DM considers you could have come across such knowledge in the past.
You can learn a new language or gain a proficiency with a new tool or skill ten times faster than the others.
You always know which way is north. You always know the number of hours left before the next sunrise or sunset.

entries.name: Perilious Instincts
entries: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice:

items: Strength Score
Dexterity Score
Constitution Score
Armor Class
Current Hit Points

subclassFeature.name: Vengeful Strike
entries: {@i 11th-level Sleuth feature}
Starting at 11th level, you can fuel your strikes with vengeance of justice. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@dice 6d8} psychic damage to the target.
This damage increases to {@dice 8d8} when you reach 15th level in this class.
Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Discombobulate
entries: {@i 15th-level Sleuth feature}

At 15th level you gain the ability to precisely predict your enemy’s moves and thwart them. When you see a creature about to roll an attack against you, you may use your reaction to counterattack that creature, if you hit, the target’s attack fails and they must roll a wisdom saving throw against your spell DC, on a fail the target is stunned until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Winter Walker
entries: {@i The night is cold and full of terrors... and I am one of them!}

Winter Walkers hone their craft in the bleak and frozen wilds of places. These ruthless, rimed Rangers hunt monsters that haunt arctic wastelands, eventually becoming frigid terrors themselves. Winter Walkers are well versed in the phenomena of tundra and ice capped mountains, including the latent magic of fallen ancient cities, endemic monsters like yetis and crag cats, and the rising threat of Underdark invaders. Due to their cold pragmatism, terrifying magic, and mastery of the region, Winter Walkers are regarded with equal parts respect and fear. Some people say that Winter Walkers' frequent exposure to malignant entities gives them their fearsome powers. Many nomads, on the other hand, believe that nature spirits bestow on Winter Walkers a unique curse.

subclassFeature.name: Winter Walker Magic
entries: {@i 3rd-level Winter Walker feature}
When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Winter Walker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Frigid Explorer
entries: {@i 3rd-level Winter Walker feature}
You gain the following benefits.
{@b Biting Cold.} Damage from your weapon attacks, Ranger spells, and Ranger features ignores Resistance to Cold damage.
{@b Frost Resistance.} You have Resistance to Cold damage.

{@b Polar Strikes.} When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can deal an extra {@dice 1d4} Cold damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. When you reach Ranger level 11, this extra damage increases to {@dice 1d6}.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter's Rime
entries: {@i 3rd-level Winter Walker feature}
Ice rimes you and your prey, protecting you and slowing them. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark}, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Ranger level.
Additionally, while a creature is marked by your {@spell Hunter's Mark}, it can't take the {@action Disengage} action.

subclassFeature.name: Fortifying Soul
entries: {@i 7th-level Winter Walker feature}

Your experience surviving harrowing environments allows you to bolster your allies in addition to yourself. As an action, choose a number of creatures you can see equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one). Each chosen creature regains Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Ranger level and has Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition for 1 hour.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Chilling Retribution
entries: {@i 11th-level Winter Walker feature}

When a creature hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Stunned} condition until the end of your next turn. While the target is {@condition Stunned}, its Speed is reduced to 0 feet.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Frozen Haunt
entries: {@i 15th-level Sleuth feature}

When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark}, you can adopt a ghostly, snowy form. This form lasts until the spell ends, and while you are in this form, you gain the following benefits. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Improved Mastermind
entries: {@i The sharpest weapon you wield is not the blade but your mind.}

Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this archetype, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favors are some of your favorite treasures.

entries.subclassFeature: Master of Tactics and Deceit|Rogue|PHB|Jay's Mastermind|JaysGuideToMaa|3

subclassFeature.name: Master of Intrigue
entries: {@i 3rd-level Mastermind feature}

When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with the {@item disguise kit}, the {@item forgery kit}, and one {@item gaming set} of your choice. You also learn two {@quickref languages} of your choice.

Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute, allowing you to pass yourself off as a native speaker of a particular land, provided that you know the language.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Tactics and Deceit
entries: {@i 3rd-level Mastermind feature}

Starting at 3rd level, you can use the {@action Help} action as a bonus action. When you use the {@action Help} action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target of that attack can be within 30 feet of you, rather than 5 feet of you, if the target can see or hear you.
Additionally, you gain proficiency in {@skill Persuasion} and {@skill Intimidation}, if you don't have it already.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Assistance
entries: {@i 3rd-level Mastermind feature}

When you use the {@action Help} action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, you may choose to give them tactical assistance. On their next hit before your next turn, they can add extra damage to their attack equal to your sneak attack. You can use this feature only once that you regain on finishing a long rest.
On reaching 9th level in Rogue class, you can use this trait twice and regain all expended uses on finishing a long rest.
On reaching 15th level in Rogue class, you can use this trait thrice and regain all expended uses on finishing a long rest.
On reaching 19th level in Rogue Class, you can use this trait four times and regain all expended uses on finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Observation
entries: {@i 3rd-level Mastermind feature}
Starting at 3rd level, you gain the ability to study a creature quickly during combat and discern how best to hurt it. As a bonus action, target one creature you can see within 60 feet of you.

The creature makes a Charisma Saving Throw (DC=8 + your Intelligence Modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). If the creature fails, you learn either damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities that creature may have and what they are. You must choose the trait you want to learn before the creature rolls the saving throw. If the creature is hidden from divination magic, you sense that it has no damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to half of your Sneak Attack die, rounded up. You regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.
On reaching 13th level in Rogue class, you can learn two traits at once. On reaching 17th level in Rogue class, you can learn all traits at once.

subclassFeature.name: Insightful Manipulator
entries: {@i 9th-level Mastermind feature}

Starting at 9th level, if you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics of your choice:

items: Intelligence Score
Wisdom Score
Charisma Score
Class Levels (if any)

entries: At the DM's option, you might also realize you know a piece of the creature's history or one of its personality traits, if it has any. Additionally, you gain advantage for {@skill Persuasion} and {@skill Intimidation} checks made against that creature if it can understand you.

subclassFeature.name: Mind Palace
entries: {@i 13th-level Mastermind feature}

Beginning at 13th level, no matter how dire a situation can be, you can focus your mind to quickly assess, analyze, and ascertain the best course of action utilizing the abilities of your allies. As an action, you may consult your mind palace (Dungeon Master) to review any detail that you and your allies might missed to notice, any important detail that you may not recalling or remembering, or any tactic that you haven't considered deploying.
The Dungeon Master may ask you to make a number of skill check rolls to determine what suggestions to offer you.
The Dungeon Master may ask you to make a number of skill check rolls to determine what suggestions to offer you.

subclassFeature.name: Soul of Deceit
entries: {@i 17th-level Mastermind feature}
Starting at 17th level, you have perfected the craft of wrapping your lies within a complex web of logic and reason, insight checks against you are made with disadvantage.
Additionally, your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means, unless you allow it.
You can present false thoughts by making a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check contested by the mind reader's Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.

Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates you are being truthful, if you so choose, and you can't be compelled to tell the truth by magic.

subclassFeature.name: Mind Fortress
entries: {@i 17th-level Mastermind feature}

Starting at 17th level, you have made your mind a fortress against magical and psychic manipulation. You roll Any Wisdom or Intelligence Saving Throw forced by a magic spell or a similar effect is rolled with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Spellfire Sorcery
entries: {@i Wield Raw Magic.}

Your innate power stems from the source of magic itself: the Weave. This connection manifests as a rare ability known as spellfire, and you surge with radiant bursts of this raw magic. Your talent with spellfire allows you to heal allies, sear enemies, and absorb powerful spells.

Wielders of spellfire tend to have a penchant for wandering. Many travel between cosmopolitan settlements, such as those along the Sword Coast, and wield their magic in service of the common good. Others realize their own strange powers by roaming equally strange lands, from the magic-blasted wastes of the desert of Anauroch to the god-touched wilds of the Old Empires. Wherever they go in the Realms, spellfire Sorcerers are courted by factions with interests in the arcane arts, such as the Harpers, Cult of the Dragon, and Red Wizards.

subclassFeature.name: Spellfire Burst
entries: {@i 3rd-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}

When you spend at least 1 Sorcery Point as part of a Magic action or a Bonus Action on your turn, you can unleash one of the following magical effects of your choice. You can do so only once per turn.
{@b Bolstering Flames.} You or one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself gains Temporary Hit Points equal to {@dice 1d4} plus your Charisma modifier.
{@b Radiant Fire.} One creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself takes {@dice 1d4} Fire or Radiant damage (your choice).

subclassFeature.name: Spellfire Spells
entries: {@i 3rd-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}
When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Spellfire Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Spellfire Spells
rows: {@spell Cure Wounds}, {@spell Guiding Bolt}, {@spell Lesser Restoration}, {@spell Scorching Ray}

subclassFeature.name: Innate Sorcery
entries: {@i 6th-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}
An event in your past left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with a simmering magic. As a Bonus Action, you can unleash that magic for 1 minute, during which you gain the following benefits:

items: The spell save DC of your Sorcerer spells increases by 1.
You have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast.

entries: You can use this feature twice, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Absorb Spells
entries: {@i 6th-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}
You always have the {@spell Counterspell} spell prepared.
Additionally, whenever you thwart a spell by a hostile creature by casting Counterspell, you regain {@dice 1d4} Sorcery Points.

subclassFeature.name: Honed Spellfire
entries: {@i 14th-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}
Your Spellfire Burst improves. You add your Sorcerer level to the Temporary Hit Points gained from Bolstering Flames, and the damage of your Radiant Fire increases to {@dice 1d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Crown of Spellfire
entries: {@i 18th-level Spellfire Sorcerer Feature.}

When you use Innate Sorcery, you can alter it and infuse yourself with the essence of spellfire, gaining the following benefits while this use of Innate Sorcery is active. Once you use this feature to alter Innate Sorcery, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

{@b Burning Life Force.} Once per turn when you are hit by an attack roll, you can expend a number of Hit Point Dice, up to a maximum equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). Roll the expended dice, and reduce the amount of damage from that attack equal to the total rolled.
{@b Flight.} You gain a Fly Speed of 60 feet and can hover.

{@b Spell Avoidance.} When you’re subjected to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the save and only half damage if you fail. You can’t use this benefit if you have the Incapacitated condition.

entries.name: Age
entries: Maarut mature at the age of eighteen (18) years old and have a natural average lifespan of 120 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Maarut gravitate towards good. They have immense respect for the law until they find corruption or cannibalization of the system which may propel them to stir chaos to restore a benevolent order. But such nature can also be clouded with poor judgement and twisted towards evil alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Medium. Maarut generally grow up to 4 to 6.5 feet or roughly 120 cm to 200 cm

entries.name: Third Appendage
entries: You have a long tail that can be nimble, strong, and sturdy. With your tail, you may:

items.name: Grab and Hurl
items.entry: Pick up a blunt object (up to 10 pounds) within reach (5 ft) and then throw it as a ranged attack as a bonus action towards a target (range 30ft/60ft) to deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage on hit. Or pick up a blunt object (up to 30 pounds) within reach and make a ranged attack as an attack action at a target (range 15ft/30ft) to deal 1d10 bludgeoning damage on hit. You may use Dexterity modifier for Grab and Throw attack rolls.

items.name: Grappling Tail
items.entry: As an attack action, you may use your free tail to attempt a grapple a creature, large size or smaller, that is behind you. A creature grappled by your tail stays behind your back, and thus any attack against that creature made by you must be made with disadvantage. The creature may break grapple as an action by contesting an athletics or acrobatics check against your athletics check on their turn. You may grapple another creature within range using a free hand as stated on PHB p195 while having a creature grappled by your tail.

items.name: Constrict and Trip
items.entry: As an attack action, make a grapple check with your free tail against a target, large size or smaller, within melee range. If you succeed, the target falls prone and takes bludgeoning damage equals to your strength modifier. If you fail, the next attack against you gains advantage.

items.name: Nimble Yank
items.entry: When located within 5 feet of a medium or large stationary object that your tail can wrap around, such as a pillar or tree that weighs more than you, you may use the said object to slingshot yourself up to 10 feet past it as a reaction after an attack is made against you.

entries.name: Primal Instincts
entries: You gain proficiency with two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Stealth}, and {@skill Sleight of Hand}.

entries.name: Unexpected Talents
entries: You gain proficiency in one additional language. You also gain proficiency in one {@item musical instrument|PHB} or one {@item gaming set|PHB}.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice, that your Dungeon Master approves.

Wild at heart, wild of nature, the simian cousins of Humans, Maarut are deceptive in appearance. For people oblivious of their existence, they might appear unrestrained agents of chaos. But there is a method to their madness, often backed by a font of intelligence and or wisdom. They are fond of art, music, and revelry but also take pride in performing their responsibilities and duties. Wit, intellectualism, and wisdom draws them into conversation, but they also typically good at hiding their wariness underneath a cloak of amicability.

entries.name: Nature
entries: They serve the order and justice with loyalty, but are always a step away to unleash the chaos burning inside their core if it helps the needy. However, such dangerous devotion to altruism often leads to them poking hornet's nests in the worst way possible. But such daring and hardheaded fortitude has also led many such Maarut to perform supernatural feats. Due to such core principles and internal nature, many Maarut lean towards Chaotic Good. But such nature can also be clouded with poor judgement and twisted towards evil alignments.

entries.name: Life on Maa
entries: They serve the order and justice with loyalty, but are always a step away to unleash the chaos burning inside their core if it helps the needy. However, such dangerous devotion to altruism often leads to them poking hornet's nests in the worst way possible. But such daring and hardheaded fortitude has also led many such Maarut to perform supernatural feats. Due to such core principles and internal nature, many Maarut lean towards Chaotic Good.

entries.name: Maarut Feminine Names
entries: Anjana, Rooma, Sugri, Taara, Makari, Neela, Saari, Joon, Sushena, Dariyaa.

entries.name: Maarut Masculine Names
entries: Angad, Baali, Greev, Feeki, Nall, Neel, Kesari, Soon, Jabari, Baakoo.

entries.name: Age
entries: Like humans, naga reach adulthood in their late teens. They show no signs of aging beyond that point except for growing larger, so in theory, a naga could live well over a century.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most naga are either neutral or good in alignment.

entries.name: Size
entries: Naga stand about 5 feet tall when upright, but the total length of their bodies, head to tail, ranges from 8 to 10 feet. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your fanged maw and constricting serpentine body are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with your bite, you deal piercing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, and your target must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, the target takes 1d4 poison damage. If you hit with your constrict attack, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier, and the target is grappled (escape DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and you can’t constrict another target.

entries.name: Speed Burst
entries: By lowering your body to the ground and propelling yourself with your arms, you can move more quickly for a time. As a bonus action on your turn, if you have both hands free, you can increase your walking speed by 5 feet until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Poison Immunity
entries: You are immune to poison damage and can’t be poisoned.

entries.name: Poison Affinity
entries: You gain proficiency with the poisoner’s kit.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice, that your Dungeon Master approves.

Naga resemble enormous snakes with shoulders, arms, and a torso that resembles a humanoid form. They typically hold their heads and torsos off the ground while moving, but they can increase their speed by lowering their bodies and using their hands for extra propulsion. They adorn their torsos with armor, jewelry, and a vague nod toward the clothing worn by other races. Male naga have broad hoods, wider than their shoulders, while females have narrower hoods and longer faces.

entries.name: Life on Maa
entries: Naga believe in a principle called the Purushaarth or Spiritual Harmony, which describes a perfect balance between the body and the mind. Finding that balance, as they understand it, is a sure path to ensure success in every trial regarding every facet of life.
In the same way, the naga believe that fate and actions exist in interdependence, and one should strive to give their best effort with Nishkaam (selfless) intentions as does Shunya, their patron god.

entries.name: Naga Feminine Names
entries: Mansha, Kali, Psin, Mere, Seger, Neheb, Renenutet, Wadee, Dusa, Dua, Korra, Sora, Naini, Ulupi, Yogini.

entries.name: Naga Masculine Names
entries: Astik, Shesh, Kurm, Aahi, Vritra, Ning, Shizd, Vasuki, Pep, Mushu, Kaal, Takshak, Karkotak, Yogi.

spell.name: Alustriel's Mooncloak
entries: For the duration, moonlight fills a 20-foot radius area originating from you with dim light. While in that area, you and your allies have {@quickref Cover||3||half cover} and resistance to Cold, Lightning, and Radiant damage.
While the spell lasts, you can use one of the following options, ending the spell immediately:

items.name: Liberation
entries: When you fail a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition frightened}, {@condition grappled}, or {@condition restrained} condition, you can take a reaction to succeed on the save instead.

items.name: Respite
entries: As an action, you or an ally within the area regains Hit Points equal to {@dice 4d10} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

spell.name: Arcane Vigor
entries: You tap into your life force to heal yourself. Roll one or two of your unexpended Hit Point Dice, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll's total plus your spellcasting ability modifier. Those dice are then expended.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The number of unexpended Hit Dice you can roll increases by one for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Backlash

time.condition: which you take in response to taking damage

entries: You ward yourself against destructive energy, reducing the damage taken by {@dice 4d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

If the triggering damage was from a creature within range, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. The creature takes {@damage 4d6} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage reduction and Force damage from this spell both increase by {@scaledamage 4d6|4-9|1d6} for every spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Befuddlement

components.m: a key ring with no keys

entries: You blast the mind of a creature that you can see within range. The target makes an Intelligence saving throw.

On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 10d12} Psychic damage and can't cast spells or take the  action. At the end of every 30 days, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on a success. The effect can also be ended by the {@spell greater restoration}, {@spell heal}, or {@spell wish} spell.
On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

spell.name: Cacophonic Shield
entries: Thunderous reverberations fill a 10-foot radius originating from you for the duration. Whenever the radius enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the radius or ends its turn there, the creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d6} Thunder damage and has the {@condition deafened} condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. A creature makes this save only once per turn. When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it.
In addition, you have resistance to Thunder damage, and ranged attack rolls against you are made with disadvantage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d6|3-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Conjure Constructs
entries: You conjure a group of intangible, orderly spirits that appear as a Medium group of modrons or other Constructs in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The spirits last for the duration. When you cast this spell and as a  action on subsequent turns, you can command the spirits to target one creature or object you can see within 5 feet of the spirits and create one of the following effects:

items.name: Clockwork Force
entries: The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

items.name: Orderly Ward
entries: The target gains Temporary Hit Points equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
When you move on your turn, you can also move the spirits up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage and Temporary Hit Points both increase by {@scaledamage 3d6|3-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Death Armor

m.text: an onyx worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: For the duration, an inky aura surrounds one creature you touch. The target has advantage on Death Saving Throw, and once per turn, when a creature within 5 feet of the target hits it with a melee attack roll, the attacker takes {@damage 2d4} Necrotic damage.

spell.name: Deryan's Helpful Homunculi

m.text: powdered gemstones worth 100+ GP, which the spell consumes, and one set of Artisan's Tools with which you have proficiency

entries: You summon a group of helpful spirits, which lasts for the duration. The spirits appear as homunculi or as another Construct of your choice but are intangible and invulnerable, and they are considered to have proficiency in the {@skill arcana} skill and with the set of {@item artisan's tools} used in the spell's casting.
If you are crafting an item, the spirits function as a single assistant for your crafting, halving the crafting time.

spell.name: Dirge
entries: Deathly power fills a 60-foot radius originating from you for the duration.

When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it. Any other creature can't regain hit point while in the radius. Whenever the radius enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the radius or ends its turn there, the creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage and has the {@condition prone} condition. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and its speed is halved. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

spell.name: Doomtide
entries: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of inky fog within range. The fog is magical darkness and lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as the one created by the {@spell gust of wind} spell) disperses it, ending the spell.

Each creature in the Sphere when it appears makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d6} Psychic damage and subtracts {@dice 1d6} from its saving throws until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. A creature also makes this save when the Sphere moves into its space, when it enters the Sphere, or when it ends its turn inside the Sphere. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
The Sphere moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns.

spell.name: Drawmij's Instant Summons
entries: You touch the sapphire used in the casting and an object weighing 10 pounds or less whose longest dimension is 6 feet or less. The spell leaves an {@condition invisible} mark on that object and invisibly inscribes the object's name on the sapphire. Each time you cast this spell, you must use a different sapphire.
Thereafter, you can take a  action to speak the object's name and crush the sapphire. The object instantly appears in your hand regardless of physical or planar distances, and the spell ends.
If another creature is holding or carrying the object, crushing the sapphire doesn't transport it, but instead you learn who that creature is and where that creature is currently located.

spell.name: Elementalism
entries: You exert control over the elements, creating one of the following effects within range.

entries.name: Beckon Air
entries: You create a breeze strong enough to ripple cloth, stir dust, rustle leaves, and close open doors and shutters, all in a 5-foot cube. Doors and shutters being held open by someone or something aren't affected.

entries.name: Beckon Earth
entries: You create a thin shroud of dust or sand that covers surfaces in a 5-foot-square area, or you cause a single word to appear in your handwriting in a patch of dirt or sand.

entries.name: Beckon Fire
entries: You create a thin cloud of harmless embers and colored, scented smoke in a 5-foot cube. You choose the color and scent, and the embers can light candles, torches, or lamps in that area. The smoke's scent lingers for 1 minute.

entries.name: Beckon Water
entries: You create a spray of cool mist that lightly dampens creatures and objects in a 5-foot cube. Alternatively, you create 1 cup of clean water either in an open container or on a surface, and the water evaporates in 1 minute.

entries.name: Sculpt Element
entries: You cause dirt, sand, fire, smoke, mist, or water that can fit in a 1-foot cube to assume a crude shape (such as that of a creature) for 1 hour.

races.name: Half-Elf (Variant; Moon Elf or Sun Elf Descent)

spell.name: Elminster's Effulgent Spheres
entries: Six chromatic spheres orbit you for the duration.
While the spheres are present, you can expend spheres to create the following effects:

items.name: Absorb Energy
entries: When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage, you can take a reaction to expend one sphere and give yourself resistance to the triggering damage type until the start of your next turn.

items.name: Energy Blast
entries: As a bonus action, you send one sphere hurtling toward a target within 120 feet of yourself. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d6} Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (your choice). Regardless of whether you hit, the sphere is expended.
The spell ends early if you have no more spheres remaining.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The number of spheres increases by 1 for every spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Elminster's Elusion
entries: Arcane wards protect you against magic for the duration. You have advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects. Additionally, if you succeed on a saving throw against a spell or magical effect and would normally take half as much damage, you instead take no damage.

spell.name: Fount of Moonlight
entries: A cool light wreathes your body for the duration, emitting bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.
